### Fix 2

Rule [family:Select]:
## Select Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Select activeItemId={...} activeMenu={...} containsDrilldown={...} containsFlyout={...} drilldownItemPath={...} drilledInMenus={...} focusTimeoutDelay={...} id={...}>
    <SelectList isAriaMultiselectable={...}>
      <SelectOption actions={...} component={...} description={...} direction={...} download={...} drilldownMenu={...} flyoutMenu={...} hasCheckbox={...}>
        <SelectList />
        <Select />
      </SelectOption>
    </SelectList>
    <SelectGroup innerRef={...} label={...} labelHeadingLevel={...} titleId={...} />
  </Select>
```

Props that stay on <Select>: activeItemId, activeMenu, containsDrilldown, containsFlyout, drilldownItemPath, drilledInMenus, focusTimeoutDelay, id, innerRef, isMenuDrilledIn, isNavFlyout, isOpen, isPlain, isRootMenu, isScrollable, maxMenuHeight, menuHeight, onActionClick, onDrillIn, onDrillOut, onGetMenuHeight, onOpenChange, onOpenChangeKeys, onSelect, onToggleKeydown, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, parentMenu, popperProps, role, selected, shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen, shouldFocusToggleOnSelect, shouldPreventScrollOnItemFocus, toggle, variant, zIndex

Add imports: SelectGroup, SelectList, SelectOption from '@patternfly/react-core'

Prop type changes:

  onSelect: (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: string | number) => void -> (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: SelectOptionProps['value']) => void
  onSelect: (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: string | number) => void -> (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: SelectOptionProps['value']) => void
  popperProps: SelectPopperProps -> PopperOptions
  popperProps: SelectPopperProps -> PopperOptions

### Fix 3
Line: 249
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: TextInputGroupUtilities

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  248            <TextInputGroupUtilities>
  249              <Button onClick={onClearButtonClick} variant={ButtonVariant.plain}>
  250                <TimesIcon />

```

### Fix 4
Line: 225
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>

Incident context:
  componentName: MenuToggle
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: __ComponentReturn__

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: MenuToggle
Prop: icon

```
  226        isExpanded={isOpen}

```

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-textinputgroupmain]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <TextInputGroupMain> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</TextInputGroupMain>

### Fix 6
Line: 11
Rule [sd-test-select-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Select is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Select renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Select may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

Incident context:
  importedName: Select
  localName: Select
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Code context:
```
   10    MenuToggleElement,
   11    Select,
   12    SelectList,

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260608T145537/kubevirt-plugin/source/src/utils/components/SelectTypeahead/SelectTypeahead.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Line 249): `Button.variant` type changed — added `'stateful'` to the union. The code uses `ButtonVariant.plain` which is still valid. **No code change needed** — the value `plain` exists in both old and new types.

**Fix 2** (Line 266): `Select.onSelect` type changed — the `value` parameter changed from `number | string | undefined` to `SelectOptionProps['value']`. Looking at the current `onSelect` handler (line 102-126), the signature uses `value: number | string | undefined`. Need to update the type to match `SelectOptionProps['value']`. `SelectOptionProps` is already imported (line 14).

**Fix 3** (Line 249): Button icon-children-to-prop. The `<TimesIcon />` inside `<Button>` needs to be moved to the `icon` prop. Since it's the only child, the Button becomes self-closing.

**Fix 4** (Line 225): MenuToggle icon-children-to-prop. Looking at lines 225-255, the MenuToggle contains a `<TextInputGroup>` as children, not an icon. The children are a text input group, not an icon element. The MenuToggle's children here are functional content (typeahead text input), not an icon. **No icon change needed** — the children are not icon elements.

**Fix 5** (Line 233): TextInputGroupMain icon-children-to-prop. Looking at line 233-244, the `TextInputGroupMain` already has `icon={<SearchIcon />}` (line 235) and has no icon children. **Already applied** — the icon is already a prop.

**Fix 6** (Line 11): Test impact — behavioral change in Select's rendered Popper (appendTo default changed). This is informational about test files. The main source file doesn't need changes for this.

Let me now apply the changes:

No test files found. Now let me carefully reason through each fix and apply them:

**Fix 1** (Line 249, Button.variant type changed): The type added `'stateful'` to the union. The code uses `ButtonVariant.plain` which is still a valid value in the new type. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2** (Line 266, Select.onSelect type changed): The `value` parameter type changed from `number | string` to `SelectOptionProps['value']`. The current `onSelect` handler (lines 102-105) has `value: number | string | undefined`. Since `SelectOptionProps` is already imported, I need to update the handler's type signature.

**Fix 3** (Line 249, Button icon-children-to-prop): `<TimesIcon />` is the only child of `<Button>`. Move it to the `icon` prop and make Button self-closing.

**Fix 4** (Line 225, MenuToggle icon-children-to-prop): Looking at lines 224-256, the MenuToggle's children are `<TextInputGroup>` — this is functional content, not an icon. The MenuToggle with `variant="typeahead"` uses TextInputGroup as its content, which is the correct v6 pattern. **No icon change needed** — children are not icons.

**Fix 5** (Line 233, TextInputGroupMain icon-children-to-prop): Line 235 already has `icon={<SearchIcon />}` as a prop, and there are no icon children inside TextInputGroupMain. **Already applied.**

**Fix 6** (Line 11, Select behavioral change): This is informational about test impact. No source code change needed in the main file.

Now let me apply Fix 2 and Fix 3:
Now let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct. Let me also verify that the `selectOption` helper function on line 94 and line 123 still work correctly with the new `SelectOptionProps['value']` type. The `value` parameter flows from `onSelect` into `selectOption` and string comparisons — `SelectOptionProps['value']` is typed as `string | number` in PF v6, so the existing logic remains compatible.

Let me also check that `TimesIcon` import is still needed (it is — used in the `icon` prop on line 249):

Good — `TimesIcon` is still used on line 249 in the `icon` prop, so the import is correctly retained. The file is complete and correct.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| **Fix 1** | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The code uses `ButtonVariant.plain` which is still a valid value in the new type union (only `'stateful'` was added). |
| **Fix 2** | `Select.onSelect` type changed | **Applied** | Updated the `onSelect` handler's `value` parameter type from `number \| string \| undefined` to `SelectOptionProps['value'] \| undefined` (line 104). `SelectOptionProps` was already imported. |
| **Fix 3** | Button icon-children-to-prop | **Applied** | Moved `<TimesIcon />` from Button's children to the `icon` prop and made Button self-closing (line 249). Changed from `<Button ...><TimesIcon /></Button>` to `<Button ... icon={<TimesIcon />} />`. |
| **Fix 4** | MenuToggle icon-children-to-prop | **No change needed** | The MenuToggle's children are `<TextInputGroup>` (functional typeahead content), not an icon element. The `variant="typeahead"` pattern correctly uses TextInputGroup as children. No icon migration applicable. |
| **Fix 5** | TextInputGroupMain icon-children-to-prop | **Already applied** | The `<SearchIcon />` is already passed via the `icon` prop (line 235), not as children. No change needed. |
| **Fix 6** | Select transitive behavioral changes (Popper appendTo) | **No change needed** | This is an informational/test-impact rule about Popper's `appendTo` default changing. No source code change required in the main file. No companion test files were found. |
